dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal prohibits shorting at gap-down open after breakdown. Interpreted as: (1) stock has broken down 5-7% (using returns <= -0.05 as proxy for lower bound), (2) gap-down opening (open < prev_close). Entry conditions capture the scenario to AVOID shorting. Exit uses pullback to support via SMA_20 crossover. Direction set to LONG because the rule is about waiting for pullback before entering long in uptrending gap market (complementary scenario mentioned). Parameters: breakdown_percentage approximated via returns, gap_down via open/prev_close comparison.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal prohibits shorting at gap-down open after breakdown. Interpreted as: (1) stock has broken down 5-7% (using returns <= -0.05 as proxy for lower bound), (2) gap-down opening (open < prev_close). Entry conditions capture the scenario to AVOID shorting. Exit uses pullback to support via SMA_20 crossover. Direction set to LONG because the rule is about waiting for pullback before entering long in uptrending gap market (complementary scenario mentioned). Parameters: breakdown_percentage approximated via returns, gap_down via open/prev_close comparison.

source_rule_text: In a trending market with significant gap-down opening after a prior breakdown, do not short at the open; correspondingly, in an uptrending gapping market, wait for a pullback to support before entering long.
